topic: King's Chamber 10th harmonic — EXP-054 post-hoc audit (obscure category O) + legitimate-novelty carve-out review
queue_reason: EXP-054 post-hoc audit flagged WIN-003 as obscure (category O). Priority queue review to determine whether legitimate-novelty carve-out applies.
our_argument_summary: WIN-003 verdict MISLEADING is correct and the legitimate-novelty carve-out does NOT apply. The EXP-054 audit correctly flagged this WIN as 'obscure' (category O), but after targeted review: (1) No derivation exists — the dome says '117.0 Hz observed — consistent with dome resonance harmonic structure' without any formula, equation, or dome parameter producing 117 Hz. Criterion 1 of the carve-out (unique dome-framework derivation) fails unambiguously. (2) Reid's 117 Hz measurement is publicly indexed on cymascope.com since ~1997 and widely circulated in alternative science communities — plausibly known to the dome author. Criterion 2 (not plausibly known at write-time) is borderline at best. (3) No derivation was published with the value. Criterion 3 fails. Since all three criteria must hold and at least two fail, the carve-out does not apply. The FLAG text in detail_verdict_text should be replaced with a resolution paragraph.

criterion_1_unique_derivation.evidence: The dome site says ONLY: 'Source: Reid 1997. 117.0 Hz observed — consistent with dome resonance harmonic structure.' No formula, no equation, no derivation path from dome parameters (disc_radius=20015 km, firmament_height=9086 km, etc.) to 117 Hz exists anywhere in the dome model. The phrase 'consistent with dome resonance harmonic structure' is asserted without defining what that structure is. Quick computation: c/(2*firmament_height) = 16.5 Hz, c/(2*disc_radius) = 7.5 Hz — neither produces 117 Hz from dome geometry. The only way to reach 117 Hz is 10 × 11.7 Hz (Tesla, WIN-001), which is a numerical coincidence, not a derivation.

criterion_2_not_known_at_write_time.evidence: Reid's 117 Hz measurement has been publicly available on cymascope.com since the late 1990s. It has been cited in numerous popular science articles, YouTube presentations, and alternative science forums for over 25 years. A Google search for 'King's Chamber resonance frequency' readily returns 117 Hz. While the value is not in undergraduate acoustics textbooks (hence the 'O' classification), it is well-known in the specific research community that the dome author evidently draws from (alternative physics, sacred geometry, ancient structure analysis). 'Not plausibly known to the author at write-time' is not credible given the dome author's evident research breadth across similar topics.

criterion_3_derivation_published_with_va.evidence: The dome model provides no derivation alongside the 117 Hz value. It simply states the value was 'observed' and claims consistency. A legitimate carve-out requires 'the derivation and the value were published together, with the derivation step sufficient to distinguish this framework from the standard model's.' No derivation step exists.

carve_out_analysis.verdict: Carve-out does NOT apply. At least 2 of 3 criteria fail unambiguously (criteria 1 and 3). Criterion 2 also likely fails. The post_hoc tag stands. The FLAG text should be resolved.

No monitor.py domain for WIN-003 (confirmed by grep)
No inject_ai_layer.py reference found in current raw-text
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: None. The dome model observes 117 Hz (from Reid 1997) and asserts consistency with an undefined 'dome resonance harmonic structure.' No calculation, no formula, no derivation.
data_sources_used: Reid 1997 (cymascope.com — acoustic experiment on King's Chamber sarcophagus)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: 'Consistent with dome resonance harmonic structure' — undefined, unfalsifiable. Any frequency could be 'consistent' with an unspecified harmonic structure.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. Our review correctly states no monitor.py domain exists and the code never computes this claim.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py domain for WIN-003. The value 117.0 Hz exists only as a static assertion in the WIN list. No API call, no computation, no validation logic.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The dome does not rename an existing standard physics explanation. It claims a NEW relationship (EM dome harmonics → acoustic stone resonance) that standard physics does not recognize. The claim is wrong, but it's not relabeling — it's fabricating a connection.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Reid's 117 Hz measurement was published ~1997. The dome model incorporated it as a 'WIN' decades later. No prospective prediction of 117 Hz from dome geometry exists. The legitimate-novelty carve-out review (this document) confirms the post_hoc tag stands: no unique derivation, value is publicly available, no derivation published with value.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: 117 Hz is not derivable from dome parameters. c/(2*9086km) = 16.5 Hz, c/(2*20015km) = 7.5 Hz. No dome formula produces 117 Hz. The value comes from acoustic measurement of a stone cavity.

holes_found.description: SCIENTIFIC ERROR: detail_evidence states '117 Hz is acoustic eigenmodes determined by chamber/sarcophagus geometry and the speed of sound in granite (~5,800–6,000 m/s).' This is wrong. 117 Hz is the resonance of the AIR CAVITY inside the sarcophagus, not of the granite itself. Speed of sound in granite (~5,900 m/s) in a 2.3m sarcophagus gives a fundamental frequency of ~1,283 Hz — an order of magnitude too high. The 117 Hz measurement corresponds to the speed of sound in AIR (~343 m/s) in an effective cavity length of ~1.47m (plausible for a Helmholtz resonance of the partially-open sarcophagus air volume). A dome defender could point to this error and say 'your review doesn't understand basic acoustics — why should we trust your EM analysis?' The core argument (acoustic ≠ electromagnetic) still holds, but the specific physics claim about granite speed of sound is wrong.
holes_found.recommendation: MAJOR EDIT to detail_evidence: Replace 'the speed of sound in granite (~5,800–6,000 m/s) — standard Helmholtz resonance' with something like: 'standard acoustic resonance of the air cavity within the stone enclosure — a Helmholtz-type eigenmode determined by sarcophagus geometry (internal volume, opening dimensions) and the speed of sound in air (~343 m/s). At 117 Hz, the effective resonating length is ~1.47 m, consistent with the sarcophagus interior dimensions. The granite walls provide the rigid boundary conditions but do not determine the resonant frequency (granite's own speed of sound ~5,900 m/s would give ~1,283 Hz for the 2.3m sarcophagus length — an entirely different frequency regime).' This correction strengthens the argument by showing precise acoustic understanding.

holes_found.description: FLAG TEXT IN detail_verdict_text NEEDS RESOLUTION. The EXP-054 audit appended a 'FLAGGED FOR VERDICT REVIEW' paragraph to detail_verdict_text. This was appropriate as a placeholder, but now that the carve-out review is complete, the FLAG should be replaced with a resolution statement. Leaving an unresolved FLAG in published text looks like an open question when the answer is clear: the carve-out does not apply.
holes_found.recommendation: MINOR EDIT to detail_verdict_text: Replace the 'FLAGGED FOR VERDICT REVIEW...' paragraph with a resolution paragraph, e.g.: 'Note on post-hoc classification: The EXP-054 audit flagged this WIN as potentially obscure under the legitimate-novelty carve-out of Part 3.1b. Targeted review found: (a) no derivation of 117 Hz from dome parameters exists, (b) Reid\'s measurement has been publicly indexed since ~1997, and (c) no derivation was published with the value. All three carve-out criteria fail. The post_hoc tag stands.'

holes_found.description: The 15th Schumann harmonic is closer to 117 Hz than the 10th: 15 × 7.83 = 117.45 Hz (0.38% off). Our review only checks 10 × 7.83 = 78.3 Hz. A dome defender could shift to '15th harmonic of Schumann' and get closer. Adding this and dismissing it strengthens our text.
holes_found.recommendation: OPTIONAL: Add a note that even 15 × 7.83 = 117.45 Hz (≠ 117.0) wouldn't save the claim — it would still be numerology without a physical mechanism. But this is optional — the current text is already strong on the mechanism point.

stronger_arguments.description: ACOUSTIC CALCULATION DEMONSTRATION: Actually compute what cavity geometry produces 117 Hz in air. For a Helmholtz resonator: f = (c/2π) × √(A/(V×L_eff)). For a simple standing wave in air: f = c/(2L), L = 343/(2×117) ≈ 1.47 m. This is close to the sarcophagus width (~0.9m) for a higher mode, or a composite mode of the open-topped cavity. Showing we can derive 117 Hz from sarcophagus geometry using air acoustics (no dome parameters needed) is the strongest possible rebuttal: the value is fully explained by standard cavity acoustics.

TEMPORAL GAP: Reid published in 1996-1997. The dome model's V51 claiming this as a WIN is from 2024+. There is a 27+ year gap. Even under the dome's own 'timestamped before confirming' definition, the dome model cannot claim temporal priority over a measurement published nearly three decades earlier.

kernel_of_truth.description: Reid's 117 Hz measurement of the King's Chamber sarcophagus is a real, reproducible acoustic phenomenon. The sarcophagus genuinely resonates at this frequency when excited. The numerical coincidence 117 = 10 × 11.7 is exact and would be striking to anyone who had independently derived 11.7 Hz from dome geometry. The dome author's instinct to look for harmonic relationships in resonance frequencies is a legitimate scientific heuristic — harmonics do reveal structural properties in physics.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Three fatal problems: (1) The dome model provides NO derivation of 117 Hz — it observes and retrofit-claims, violating its own 'prospective prediction' standard. (2) The 10 × 11.7 = 117 coincidence has no physical mechanism: EM dome resonance cannot drive acoustic stone vibrations without a transducer (piezoelectric effect, magnetostriction, etc.), none of which the dome model provides. (3) The 'harmonic structure' claim is unfalsifiable — ANY frequency can be expressed as some multiple of some base. The dome never specifies which base or why the 10th harmonic specifically matters. Without a derivation, a mechanism, and a specified base frequency, the claim is numerology, not physics.

specific_patches.action: Replace 'speed of sound in granite (~5,800–6,000 m/s) — standard Helmholtz resonance' with correct air-cavity resonance explanation
Replace 'FLAGGED FOR VERDICT REVIEW...' paragraph with carve-out resolution paragraph confirming post_hoc stands

deeper_analysis:
PRIORITY QUEUE REVIEW — EXP-054 POST-HOC AUDIT, OBSCURE CATEGORY O

This review was triggered by queue_id 4: the EXP-054 post-hoc audit classified WIN-003 as category 'O' (obscure) and appended a FLAG paragraph to detail_verdict_text requesting a targeted verdict review under the legitimate-novelty carve-out of Part 3.1b.

CARVE-OUT ANALYSIS SUMMARY:
The legitimate-novelty carve-out requires ALL THREE of: (1) unique dome-framework derivation, (2) data not plausibly known at write-time, (3) derivation published with value. WIN-003 fails criteria 1 and 3 unambiguously (no derivation exists AT ALL), and criterion 2 is borderline (Reid's work is publicly indexed since ~1997). Verdict: carve-out does not apply.

NEW FINDING — SCIENTIFIC ERROR IN OUR TEXT:
Our detail_evidence claims 117 Hz comes from 'speed of sound in granite (~5,800–6,000 m/s).' This is scientifically incorrect. Granite speed of sound in a 2.3m sarcophagus gives ~1,283 Hz (fundamental mode), not 117 Hz. The 117 Hz measurement is the resonance of the AIR CAVITY inside the sarcophagus (speed of sound in air ~343 m/s, effective cavity length ~1.47 m). The granite walls provide rigid boundary conditions but do not determine the resonant frequency. This error does not affect the verdict (acoustic ≠ electromagnetic regardless), but a dome defender could exploit it to undermine our credibility on physics. Recommend correcting.

VERDICT ASSESSMENT:
Misleading remains the correct verdict. The claim is a post-hoc pattern match with no derivation, no mechanism, and no prospective prediction. The carve-out review resolves the last open question. Confidence raised from 0.92 (previous c2 review) to 0.95.

ACTION ITEMS FOR DECIDER:
1. Patch detail_evidence to correct granite→air acoustic explanation (moderate priority)
2. Patch detail_verdict_text to replace FLAG paragraph with carve-out resolution (moderate priority)
3. Optionally add 15th Schumann harmonic note (117.45 Hz ≈ 117 Hz, still numerology) — low priority

citations_verified: Reid 1996-1997 cymatics experiments — cymascope.com link works, describes sarcophagus resonance work

citations_unchecked: Sarcophagus dimensions cited as '~2.3 m × 0.9 m × 1.0 m' — source not attributed in our text; should trace to Reid or Egyptology literature
Speed of sound in granite '~5,800-6,000 m/s' — correct value per CRC Handbook, but incorrectly applied to explain 117 Hz (should be speed of sound in air for cavity resonance)
